tickets.title: Set up project structure and dependencies
tickets.description:
Initialize the test project with proper directory structure and dependency management.

Tasks:
1. Create main project directory structure (src/, tests/, docs/)
2. Initialize git repository if not already initialized
3. Create requirements.txt or package.json depending on project type
4. Set up .gitignore with common patterns
5. Create a basic README.md with project overview
6. Add a LICENSE file (MIT recommended)

Acceptance criteria:
- Project has organized directory structure
- Dependency management file exists (requirements.txt or package.json)
- Git repository is initialized with proper .gitignore
- README.md contains project description and setup instructions
- LICENSE file is present
tickets.priority_rationale: Foundation setup - must be completed before any other development work can begin
tickets.notes: Choose appropriate structure based on language: Python (src/), Node.js (lib/ or src/), etc. Include virtual environment setup instructions in README if using Python.

tickets.title: Implement core utility functions
tickets.description:
Create a module with essential utility functions for data processing.

Functions to implement:
1. validate_input(data) - Validate input data structure and types
2. parse_config(filepath) - Parse configuration from JSON/YAML file
3. format_output(data, format_type) - Format data for different output types (json, csv, text)
4. log_message(level, message) - Centralized logging with levels (debug, info, warning, error)
5. retry_operation(func, max_attempts, delay) - Retry decorator for failing operations

Requirements:
- All functions must have type hints
- Comprehensive docstrings with examples
- Error handling with custom exceptions
- Unit tests for each function

Acceptance criteria:
- Utility module exists with all 5 functions
- Functions have type hints and docstrings
- Error cases are handled gracefully
- 100% test coverage for utility module
tickets.priority_rationale: Core functionality - required for other modules to function properly
tickets.notes: Consider using existing libraries: pydantic for validation (Python), joi (Node.js). For logging, use standard library logging module or winston.

tickets.title: Add comprehensive test suite with CI integration
tickets.description:
Set up a complete testing infrastructure with continuous integration.

Components:
1. Unit tests for all modules (targeting 80%+ coverage)
2. Integration tests for key workflows
3. Test fixtures and mocks for external dependencies
4. GitHub Actions or GitLab CI configuration
5. Code coverage reporting (codecov or coveralls)
6. Pre-commit hooks for running tests

CI Pipeline stages:
- Lint and format check
- Run all tests
- Generate coverage report
- Build artifacts (if applicable)

Acceptance criteria:
- Test suite covers at least 80% of codebase
- CI configuration file exists and runs on push/PR
- Coverage reports are generated automatically
- Pre-commit hooks prevent committing untested code
- All tests pass in CI environment
tickets.priority_rationale: Quality assurance - ensures reliability and catches regressions early
tickets.notes: Use pytest for Python, jest for Node.js. For pre-commit hooks: pre-commit framework (Python) or husky (Node.js). Consider using tox for testing across multiple Python versions.

tickets.title: Create documentation and usage examples
tickets.description:
Develop comprehensive documentation for the project with practical examples.

Documentation includes:
1. API reference with all public functions/classes
2. Getting started guide with installation instructions
3. Usage examples for common scenarios
4. Architecture overview diagram
5. Contributing guidelines (CONTRIBUTING.md)
6. Changelog template (CHANGELOG.md)
7. Example configurations and use cases

Format:
- Use Markdown for all documentation
- Include code examples with syntax highlighting
- Add diagrams using Mermaid or draw.io
- Keep examples simple and runnable

Acceptance criteria:
- docs/ directory exists with organized documentation
- README.md links to detailed documentation
- At least 3 working code examples are provided
- CONTRIBUTING.md explains how to contribute
- Documentation is clear and easy to follow
tickets.priority_rationale: Important for usability and adoption, but not blocking core functionality
tickets.notes: Consider using documentation generators: Sphinx (Python), JSDoc (JavaScript), or MkDocs for both. Host documentation on GitHub Pages or Read the Docs for easy access.
